title: The use of N-α-Pyridyl-N-Benzoyl Thiourea as a Selective Reagent for Palladium
author: P. K. HALDAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chemical Laboratory, Presidency College, Calcutta 

  Manuscript received 7 June 1977, revised 12 December 1977, accepted 17 May 1978  

 N-α-Pyridyl-N'-benzoyl thiourea has been successfully employed in the determination of palladium by both gravimetry and spectrophotometry. The organic reagent forms a deep brown precipitate with palladium(ll) at room temperature in the range of acidity from 1.6(N) to 0.08(N) with respect to hydrochloric acid, which can be weighed as Pd(C 13 H 3 N 3 OS). Using proper masking agents the metal has been determined by the organic precipitant in the presence of almost all of the base metals and nobel metals except platinum. The deep brown complex is extracted quantitatively in the pH range 1.4 to 4.6 with a mixture of chloroform and ethanol for the spectrophotometric determination of the metal. The extract shows maximum absorbance at 346 nm, obeys Beer's law at 350 nm over the range of concentration of the metal from 1.18  µ g/ml to 9.44  µ g/ml. Palladium has been spectropbotometrically determined with the chelating agent in the presence of all of the base metals and noble metals.
